details: Concrete example to keep in view: CM remains on 10.167.2.150, an LDU first uses .153, and a later rebuilt remote agent reuses .153.
Treat this as a behavioral note for later cookbook review, not as an active backlog item or a proven universal rule.
Reviewed interactively with the operator on 2026-04-04 while defining the collaborative idea-log workflow.
notes.summary: In reusable lab deployments, if the central manager stays in place while a node IP is reused for a different role, the reused IP may need explicit cleanup of the earlier node identity on the central manager before reattachment behaves cleanly.
notes.title: Central manager cleanup may be needed when reused node IP changes role

details: Frontline playbooks should start from validated technical paths such as Service State, Host Health, Logs, Data Collection and Processing, and Distributed Node Role.
The CLI dashboard is only a front-door filter for core `ms-*` service state and should not be treated as a rich diagnostic surface.
If the core services are up, the next serious playbook move should go to Logs instead of forcing a service-state or GUI-first explanation.
Deeper runtime-owner concepts like listener ownership, route ownership, or backend handoff belong in cookbook or deeper successor layers, not frontline triage pages.
The operator explicitly rejected coaching sections about when to restart, when to escalate, what to report, or what evidence to capture because experienced engineers already own those judgments.
notes.summary: Frontline ADF successor playbooks should start from validated technical paths, keep the CLI `ms-*` service dashboard as a narrow front-door filter, and move quickly into Logs for real triage depth instead of coaching experienced engineers or drifting into R&D-depth runtime analysis.
notes.title: Frontline playbooks should start from technical paths and treat service state as a front-door filter

details: The new review surfaces are docs/specs/adf-successor-starlight-review-shell-plan-v1.md, docs/specs/adf-successor-adf-dev-headless-browser-host-plan-v1.md, and docs/specs/adf-successor-site-map-and-wireframe-review-v1.md.

adf-dev already serves the older ADF review page on 10.167.2.151:18082 but still lacks Chromium and Playwright, so remote browser review should wait for host provisioning instead of assuming it is ready.
The first paired successor review pages should be Service State and Core Service Groups by Node Role before the shell widens further.
notes.summary: The successor now has a concrete review-only publication baseline: reuse the older ADF Starlight publisher patterns, keep the successor shell path-first and cookbook-separated, publish Service State plus Core Service Groups by Node Role first, and treat adf-dev browser provisioning as a host-managed prerequisite before remote headless review.
notes.title: Successor review shell and adf-dev browser host should be finalized before implementation
